title: docs: add SEP-41 specification document
body:
## Summary
Create a specification document for the SEP-41 fungible token standard.

## Location
`docs/sep41-spec.md` (new file)

## What to Implement
Write a markdown document that covers:
- Overview of the SEP-41 standard and its relationship to ERC-20
- Full interface table: every function name, its parameters, return type, and a one-sentence description
- Storage layout used by `contracts/sep41-fungible/src/lib.rs` (`DataKey` enum entries)
- Error conditions for each function

Reference: https://github.com/stellar/stellar-protocol/blob/master/ecosystem/sep-0041.md

## Definition of Done
- [ ] `docs/sep41-spec.md` exists and covers all points above
- [ ] All links in the document resolve
- [ ] CI is green (no Rust changes required for this issue)

title: docs: add NFT specification document
body:
## Summary
Create a specification document for the SoroStandards NFT contract.

## Location
`docs/nft-spec.md` (new file)

## What to Implement
Write a markdown document that covers:
- Mint, transfer, burn, and approve interface definitions
- `DataKey` storage layout from `contracts/nft/src/lib.rs`
- Token URI scheme and metadata format
- Comparison table with ERC-721 equivalents

## Definition of Done
- [ ] `docs/nft-spec.md` exists and covers all points above
- [ ] CI is green

title: chore: add rustfmt.toml and clippy.toml configuration files
body:
## Summary
Add project-level formatter and linter configuration so all contributors produce consistent output.

## Location
- `rustfmt.toml` (repo root, new file)
- `.clippy.toml` (repo root, new file)

## What to Implement
**`rustfmt.toml`** — set at minimum:
```toml
edition = "2021"
max_width = 100
use_field_init_shorthand = true
```

**`.clippy.toml`** — set at minimum:
```toml
msrv = "1.78"
```

Verify that `cargo fmt --all -- --check` and `cargo clippy --all-targets` both pass after adding these files.

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Both config files exist at the repo root
- [ ] `cargo fmt --all -- --check` exits 0
- [ ] `cargo clippy --all-targets` exits 0
- [ ] CI is green

title: feat(sep41): implement full SEP-41 token interface
body:
## Summary
Implement the complete SEP-41 fungible token interface in the `sep41-fungible` contract.

## Location
`contracts/sep41-fungible/src/lib.rs`

## What to Implement
Add the following functions to `FungibleToken` (the stub already has `initialize`):

| Function | Signature |
|---|---|
| `mint` | `(env, to: Address, amount: i128)` — admin only |
| `balance` | `(env, id: Address) -> i128` |
| `transfer` | `(env, from: Address, to: Address, amount: i128)` |
| `transfer_from` | `(env, spender: Address, from: Address, to: Address, amount: i128)` |
| `approve` | `(env, from: Address, spender: Address, amount: i128, expiration_ledger: u32)` |
| `allowance` | `(env, from: Address, spender: Address) -> i128` |
| `burn` | `(env, from: Address, amount: i128)` |
| `burn_from` | `(env, spender: Address, from: Address, amount: i128)` |
| `decimals` | `(env) -> u32` |
| `name` | `(env) -> String` |
| `symbol` | `(env) -> String` |

Use `soroban_sdk::token::TokenInterface` as the trait to implement. Store balances and allowances in `env.storage().persistent()`.

## Definition of Done
- [ ] All 11 functions implemented and compile without warnings
- [ ] Unit tests cover: mint, transfer, transfer_from, approve + allowance, burn
- [ ] `cargo test -p sep41-fungible` passes
- [ ] CI is green

title: feat(nft): implement mint, transfer, and burn
body:
## Summary
Implement core NFT operations in the `nft` contract.

## Location
`contracts/nft/src/lib.rs`

## What to Implement
Add to the `NFT` contract (stub already has `initialize`):

| Function | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `mint(env, to: Address, token_uri: String) -> u64` | Increment `TotalSupply`, store `Owner(id)` and `TokenUri(id)`, return new token id |
| `transfer(env, from: Address, to: Address, token_id: u64)` | Require `from` auth, update `Owner(token_id)` |
| `burn(env, from: Address, token_id: u64)` | Require `from` auth, remove `Owner` and `TokenUri` entries |
| `owner_of(env, token_id: u64) -> Address` | Return owner or panic if not found |
| `token_uri(env, token_id: u64) -> String` | Return URI |

All storage in `env.storage().persistent()`.

## Definition of Done
- [ ] All 5 functions implemented
- [ ] Unit tests cover: mint, transfer, burn, owner_of after burn panics
- [ ] `cargo test -p nft` passes
- [ ] CI is green

title: feat(timelocks): implement deposit and claim
body:
## Summary
Complete the `timelocks` contract with `deposit` and `claim` functions.

## Location
`contracts/timelocks/src/lib.rs`

## What to Implement
The stub already has `initialize`. Add:

**`deposit(env: Env, from: Address, amount: i128)`**
- Require `from` auth
- Transfer `amount` of the stored token from `from` to the contract address using `token::Client`
- Store the deposited amount (add to any existing `Amount`)

**`claim(env: Env)`**
- Load `recipient`, `unlock_time`, `amount`
- Panic with `"not yet unlocked"` if `env.ledger().timestamp() < unlock_time`
- Transfer full balance to recipient
- Zero out stored amount

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Both functions implemented
- [ ] Unit tests cover: claim before unlock panics, claim after unlock succeeds, double-claim is a no-op
- [ ] `cargo test -p timelocks` passes
- [ ] CI is green

title: feat(multisig): implement proposal submission and execution
body:
## Summary
Implement the full M-of-N multisig proposal lifecycle.

## Location
`contracts/multisig/src/lib.rs`

## What to Implement
The stub has `initialize`. Add:

**Storage additions to `DataKey`:**
```rust
Proposal(u64),   // stores ProposalData
Approvals(u64),  // stores Vec<Address>
```

**`ProposalData` struct:**
```rust
pub struct ProposalData {
    pub callee: Address,
    pub function: Symbol,
    pub args: Vec<Val>,
    pub executed: bool,
}
```

**Functions:**

| Function | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `submit(env, callee, function, args) -> u64` | Any signer may submit; stores proposal; returns nonce; increments nonce |
| `approve(env, signer: Address, proposal_id: u64)` | Require signer auth; signer must be in `Signers`; append to `Approvals(id)` |
| `execute(env, proposal_id: u64)` | Panic if already executed or approvals < threshold; call `callee.function(args)`; mark executed |

## Definition of Done
- [ ] All 3 functions implemented
- [ ] Unit tests cover: submit, approve by non-signer panics, execute below threshold panics, successful execute
- [ ] `cargo test -p multisig` passes
- [ ] CI is green

title: feat(sep41): add TypeScript integration test for transfer flow
body:
## Summary
Write an end-to-end TypeScript integration test that deploys the `sep41-fungible` WASM and exercises the full transfer flow against a local Soroban sandbox.

## Prerequisites
Issue #4 (SEP-41 implementation) must be merged before this test can be written.

## Location
`tests/ts/sep41.test.ts` (new file)

## What to Implement
Using `@stellar/stellar-sdk` and `vitest`:

1. Deploy the compiled `sep41-fungible.wasm` to a local sandbox
2. Call `initialize` with a test admin and metadata
3. `mint` 1000 tokens to account A
4. Assert `balance(A) === 1000`
5. `transfer` 400 from A to B
6. Assert `balance(A) === 600` and `balance(B) === 400`
7. `approve` B to spend 200 from A; assert `allowance(A, B) === 200`
8. `transfer_from` 200 via B; assert final balances

Update `tests/ts/package.json` `test` script to `vitest run` if not already done.

## Definition of Done
- [ ] `tests/ts/sep41.test.ts` exists with all 8 assertions
- [ ] `npm test` in `tests/ts/` passes
- [ ] CI `ts-tests` job is green

title: feat(vesting): implement release and vested_amount
body:
## Summary
Complete the `vesting` contract with linear vesting logic and a release function.

## Location
`contracts/vesting/src/lib.rs`

## What to Implement
The stub has `initialize`. Add:

**`vested_amount(env: Env) -> i128`**
- Return 0 if `now < cliff`
- Return `total` if `now >= end`
- Otherwise return `total * (now - start) / (end - start)`

**`release(env: Env)`**
- Compute `vested_amount`
- Subtract already `Released`
- Panic with `"nothing to release"` if releasable == 0
- Transfer releasable tokens to `beneficiary` using `token::Client`
- Add releasable to `Released`

**`deposit(env: Env, from: Address, amount: i128)`**
- Require `from` auth; transfer tokens into the contract

## Definition of Done
- [ ] All 3 functions implemented
- [ ] Unit tests cover: release before cliff is 0, release at cliff, release at end, partial release mid-vesting, double-release only sends delta
- [ ] `cargo test -p vesting` passes
- [ ] CI is green

title: feat(nft): add approve and transfer_from for NFT operator pattern
body:
## Summary
Extend the `nft` contract with an operator approval pattern (analogous to ERC-721 `approve` / `transferFrom`).

## Prerequisites
Issue #5 (NFT mint/transfer/burn) must be merged first.

## Location
`contracts/nft/src/lib.rs`

## What to Implement
Add to `DataKey`:
```rust
Approved(u64),          // single-token approval -> Address
Operator(Address, Address), // owner -> operator -> bool
```

Add functions:

| Function | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `approve(env, owner: Address, spender: Address, token_id: u64, expiration_ledger: u32)` | Require owner auth; store `Approved(token_id)` with TTL |
| `set_approval_for_all(env, owner: Address, operator: Address, approved: bool)` | Require owner auth; store `Operator(owner, operator)` |
| `get_approved(env, token_id: u64) -> Option<Address>` | Return stored approval |
| `is_approved_for_all(env, owner: Address, operator: Address) -> bool` | Return stored flag |
| Update `transfer` | Also allow approved spender or operator to call transfer |

## Definition of Done
- [ ] All 4 new functions implemented; `transfer` updated
- [ ] Unit tests cover: approve then transfer by spender, operator transfer, expired approval is rejected
- [ ] `cargo test -p nft` passes
- [ ] CI is green
